VOL LANDSCAPE & ANALYTICS

The VIX at 27.5 sits in backwardation with VIX3M trading 190bp below, marking the steepest inversion in six weeks as dealers prepare for vol reset. Broad equity IV ranks have spiked to 90th percentile across SPY, EEM, and IWM while put skew reaches extreme 99th percentile levels. Credit spreads remain contained despite equity vol expansion, creating a cross-asset divergence that typically resolves within 48 hours.

VRP: Front-month IV trading 4-6 points above realized vol across major indices — aggressive vol selling premium available for patient capital
SKEW: Put skew at 99th percentile in SPY/QQQ signals maximum defensive positioning and creates asymmetric call upside reward
TERM STRUCTURE: Steep backwardation in high-beta names (AMD, META, ARM) with front-week premiums 15-20% rich to fair value

Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 15:31 ET
Two-way volatility eruption in META and ARM reveals institutional hedging ahead of Core PCE
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. META's massive 300k call spread purchase signals institutional conviction despite technical deterioration and Druckenmiller's full exit.
  • 2. The P/C divergence — index puts at 1.42 versus single-name calls at 0.92 — flags classic dispersion hedging into Friday's Core PCE.
  • 3. Negative $18B SPY gamma amplifies all directional moves as volatility sellers retreat ahead of critical inflation data.
NARRATIVE — defensive hedging mode

The most striking development is META's explosive two-way volatility demand — calls surged 282% while puts jumped 114% — driven by institutional block purchases totaling 300,000 contracts across Jan 15 strikes from 700-800. This positions for a massive move despite the stock's death cross and 19.2 technical score, suggesting smart money expects catalyst-driven volatility rather than directional momentum. The flow directly contradicts Druckenmiller's complete Q4 exit from META, indicating fresh institutional conviction at lower levels. ARM presents a similar two-way setup with calls +161% and puts +257%, though here the technical backdrop supports the call flow with an 84.3 bullish score despite the death cross. The semiconductor space shows broad volatility expansion ahead of earnings season, with AMD calls +70% despite RSI oversold conditions. This clustering suggests sector rotation positioning rather than individual stock catalysts. The broader regime reveals institutional hedging into Friday's Core PCE print. Index P/C at 1.42 reflects defensive positioning while single-name P/C at 0.92 shows stock-specific conviction — a classic dispersion trade setup. With SPY negative gamma at -$18.1B, dealers will amplify any PCE surprise moves, making the current 0.28 implied correlation potentially too low if inflation data triggers broad risk-off sentiment.

Layer 1: Sentiment -20Layer 2: GEX -$18.1BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.42/0.92Layer 9: Core PCE 1d

GREEK PRESSURE KEY
+$M = dealer buys to re-hedge
−$M = dealer sells to re-hedge
Charm: time-decay drift only. Vanna: vol-shift only. Not blended.
